context: Tests whether the agent adds a correctly structured JSON health endpoint and structured JSON request logging (observability), and implements worktree-safe port isolation using directory-path-derived ports with a proper boot poll loop — following the patterns described in the agent-readiness skill.

checklist.name: Health response has status field
checklist.description: The /health endpoint returns a JSON object containing a 'status' key (e.g., {"status": "ok", ...})

checklist.name: Health response has version or uptime
checklist.description: The /health JSON response includes at least one of: 'version' or 'uptime' fields

checklist.name: Structured JSON request logging
checklist.description: app.py emits a structured JSON log line for each request (not plain print statements) — containing at minimum method, path, and status fields

checklist.name: Port derived from directory path
checklist.description: worktree-start.sh computes the PORT value using the current working directory ($PWD or equivalent) — not a hardcoded port number

checklist.name: No hardcoded port 5000
checklist.description: worktree-start.sh does NOT use a hardcoded port 5000 (or any other fixed port number)

checklist.name: Background app start in worktree-start.sh
checklist.description: worktree-start.sh starts the Flask application in the background (using & or equivalent)

checklist.name: Poll loop in worktree-start.sh
checklist.description: worktree-start.sh polls the health endpoint in a loop (at least 10 iterations) before declaring success

checklist.name: Non-zero exit on boot failure
checklist.description: worktree-start.sh exits with a non-zero status if the app fails to start within the polling period

checklist.name: teardown.sh stops service
checklist.description: teardown.sh stops the Flask service (kills the process by PID or uses a stop command)

checklist.name: Port collision rationale documented
checklist.description: observability-notes.md explains how the port derivation prevents collisions between different worktrees on the same machine

checklist.name: Dev environment observability
checklist.description: observability-notes.md or app.py adds observability to the development/local environment (not just describing it as a production concern)
